title: Stage-1 exploration task
description: A goal-oriented, intentionally under-specified exploration task pinned to a forged/mutated corpus. The prompt asks a tool to FIND something; the evidence anchors are the adjudicated answer key, expressed against MUTATED relative paths so a tool that memorised the upstream repo cannot short-circuit them.

id.description: Stable, filesystem-safe task id. Never renumbered once shipped.

manifest_ref.description: Pins the exact forged corpus the anchors were verified against. The validator forges/locates this manifest to re-verify every anchor.

prompt.description: Under-specified, goal-oriented question (where / how / what-breaks). MUST NOT name target paths, target symbols, patch locations, or solution hints; the validator fails a prompt that leaks any evidence path segment or target identifier.

rubric.description: The grading key. Unlike the prompt, the rubric MAY name symbols/paths.

must_surface.description: Facts/locations a correct exploration answer must surface.

evidence.description: Adjudicated final evidence set: >=2 independently supporting anchors, each against a MUTATED relative path.

annotators.description: The (>=2) independent human annotators whose records back this task.

resolved.description: True once every annotator disagreement is adjudicated. A task with disagreeing annotators and resolved=false is invalid.

path.description: MUTATED relative path within the forged corpus tree.

lines.description: Exact line [n] or bounded inclusive range [start, end] (start <= end).

claim.description: What this location supports about the answer.

target_identifiers.description: Identifiers that MUST occur within the bounded evidence; consumed by downstream leakage checks.
